title: US x Iran ceasefire extended by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran announced on April 7, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between the United States and Iran, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 7 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect. 

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed two-week period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump announced on April 21 the indefinite extension of the US-Iran ceasefire—initially a two-week truce agreed April 8 via Pakistan mediation in the ongoing 2026 Iran war—conditioning it on Iran submitting a unified proposal amid Tehran's rejection of prior US drafts and reluctance for talks under naval blockade pressure. The truce holds fragilely despite Iranian FM statements blaming the US for stalled diplomacy and ongoing Strait of Hormuz tensions, with economic shocks persisting globally. Traders weigh Iran's internal fractures against potential second-round negotiations in Islamabad, whose uncertainty and demands for port access could prompt escalation or de-escalation signals.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 99.9% implied probability to no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, cementing consensus around the Fed's 3.5%-3.75% target amid resilient economic conditions. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year—exceeding the 2% goal—while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs, indicating stable labor markets without overheating or sharp cooling. The March policy hold and Fed communications reinforce this pause, with skin-in-the-game bets pricing out rate moves. Realistic challenges include surprise downside in upcoming core PCE or GDP data, though persistent inflation and steady growth strongly favor status quo; post-meeting dot plot will clarify 2026 cut expectations.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France holds a slim lead at 16.4% implied probability as trader consensus on Polymarket, boosted by March friendlies wins over Brazil (2-1, Mbappé scoring) and Colombia (3-1), propelling Les Bleus to No. 1 in FIFA rankings ahead of Spain. Spain trails closely at 15.3% after slipping from the top spot despite strong UEFA qualifiers, with both nations benefiting from manageable group stage draws—Spain facing Uruguay, Saudi Arabia, and Cape Verde; France drawn against Senegal, Norway, and a playoff winner—avoiding early clashes among seeds until knockout rounds. England (11.1%), Argentina (8.8%), and Brazil (8.5%) remain competitive amid the expanded 48-team format's unpredictability, squad depths, and prime talents like Yamal, Messi, and Vinícius Júnior, keeping the race tightly bunched post-final UEFA playoffs.

title: When will Bitcoin hit $150k?
description: When will Bitcoin hit $150k

markets.question: Will Bitcoin hit $150k by September 30?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title by 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's commanding 3-0 first-round playoff lead over the Phoenix Suns, fueled by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's playoff career-high 42 points in Game 3 and a 64-18 regular-season record as West's top seed, drives their 51.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite for the 2026 NBA Championship. San Antonio Spurs hold a resilient 3-1 edge versus Portland after Victor Wembanyama's return sparked a historic Game 4 comeback from 15 down at halftime, elevating their 15.8% odds amid strong Western Conference positioning. Boston Celtics' 3-1 series advantage over Philadelphia, including a 128-96 Game 4 rout despite Joel Embiid's return, sustains their 14.6% share, reflecting defensive prowess and repeat-title experience against a competitive Eastern field. Recent blowouts underscore OKC's depth and momentum heading into potential second-round matchups.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, reflecting his national fundraising prowess, high-profile clashes with Republican policies as California governor, and freedom from term limits after January 2027, positioning him as a seasoned executive with broad appeal. Kamala Harris trails at 8% despite recent McLaughlin polling showing her at 29% among likely voters, as markets discount her 2024 general election loss amid lingering doubts over turnout and swing-state viability. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 9% gains from progressive enthusiasm and youth, but the fragmented field—featuring governors like Shapiro and Whitmer, plus Buttigieg—signals uncertainty. Upcoming 2026 midterms, early fundraising reports, and potential endorsements could consolidate support, with historical patterns favoring incumbents or battleground performers in open primaries.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors the Iranian regime's survival past April 30, driven by its successful suppression of 2026 uprisings via massive crackdowns and executions in early April, following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death in a US-Israeli strike and swift succession by Mojtaba Khamenei. Despite economic contraction from war, sanctions, and Strait of Hormuz restrictions, Tehran announced essential-goods prioritization on April 26 and offered to lift the blockade with nuclear talks deferred as of April 27, indicating diplomatic maneuvering under IRGC dominance. No verifiable mass defections, uncontainable protests, or leadership fractures have emerged in the past 48 hours, leaving scant room for collapse before resolution. Scenarios like IRGC mutiny or sudden territorial capitulation remain highly improbable barriers to the status quo.

title: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?
description: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's "Liekinheitin" by violinist Linda Lampenius and pop star Pete Parkkonen has surged to frontrunner status at 36% implied probability on Polymarket following their commanding UMK national final victory in early April, propelled by a fierce live performance blending strings and high-energy pop that captivated fans and early jury polls. This propelled Finland past previous favorite Israel, with trader consensus reflecting strong Eurojury leads and top rankings in fan votes amid pre-party buzz. Denmark (11.5%), France (10.7%), and Greece (9.9%) hold as key challengers via solid national selection entries, but rehearsals kicking off this week in Vienna could spark shifts through staging reveals and televote-jury splits, with the grand final on May 16 determining the winner.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile ceasefire extended indefinitely by President Trump on April 22 following the initial two-week truce mediated by Pakistan on April 8, US-Iran negotiations in Islamabad have stalled over the Strait of Hormuz blockade, Iran's nuclear and ballistic missile programs, US sanctions, and Lebanon tensions. Iran's latest proposal to reopen the strait in exchange for eased restrictions met mixed signals, while on April 27, Tehran's foreign minister consulted Putin as mediators bridge gaps without face-to-face talks. Historical enmity and proxy conflicts temper trader optimism for a permanent peace deal, with upcoming diplomatic rounds in Pakistan pivotal amid escalation risks.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s rapid implementation of the MAHA agenda—including new dietary guidelines prioritizing real food, removal of toxic food dyes, SNAP reforms, lower drug prices, and HHS restructuring cutting waste—has boosted his trader consensus to 49%, reflecting strong approval for his outsider crusade against corporate influence after 14 months in office. Vice President J.D. Vance holds at 39% buoyed by CPAC straw poll wins but faces headwinds from failed Iran war negotiations, narrowing his once-dominant lead. Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 20.6% gains from high-profile diplomacy amid GOP donor efforts. The tight race persists due to Trump's endorsement uncertainty and 2026 midterms; a Vance foreign policy win or RFK scandal could separate frontrunners.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance edges California Governor Gavin Newsom as trader consensus frontrunner for the 2028 presidential winner, reflecting his 53% win in the late March CPAC straw poll for the GOP nomination and a UMass Lowell survey showing him leading Newsom 33-30 in a hypothetical general election matchup. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's post-CPAC surge to 35% has lifted his odds amid GOP primary speculation. Vance's VP achievements—like leading Iran ceasefire talks, brokering India-Pakistan peace, and courting major Republican donors—signal incumbency advantages, while Newsom's New Hampshire book tour stokes Democratic interest. This tight race persists due to the distant primaries, fragmented fields on both sides, and pending 2026 midterms, where swing state results, candidate announcements, or President Trump's endorsement could create separation.

title: Israel x Hezbollah Ceasefire extended by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the 10-day ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah announced on April 16, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between Israel and Hezbollah, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 16 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect.

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the Israeli government and Hezbollah that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed 10-day period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli government and Hezbollah. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered 10-day cessation of hostilities between Israel and Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah operations, began April 16, 2026, to enable peace negotiations, but quickly saw violations including Israeli airstrikes and Hezbollah drone attacks killing an IDF soldier. President Trump announced a three-week extension after White House talks around April 23, yet fresh escalations dominate: Netanyahu ordered vigorous strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley on April 27, prompting Hezbollah threats of suicide bomber groups and rocket responses. Ongoing ground operations, mutual breach accusations, and Israeli military frustrations underscore the truce's fragility, with traders monitoring for full collapse or renewed diplomacy amid tied Iranian tensions.

title: Indian Premier League: Delhi Capitals vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Delhi Capitals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru scheduled for April 27 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Delhi Capitals vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru
markets.description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Delhi Capitals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru scheduled for April 27 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Delhi Capitals vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Delhi Capitals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru scheduled for 2026-04-27 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Delhi Capitals vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Delhi Capitals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru scheduled for 2026-04-27 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Royal Challengers Bengaluru enter IPL 2026 Match 39 atop the points table in second place after seven games, leveraging strong form and playoffs positioning, while Delhi Capitals languish seventh with three wins following a high-scoring defeat to Punjab Kings. Key absences drive sentiment: RCB opener Phil Salt confirmed out injured—Jacob Bethell set to open with Virat Kohli—and DC paceman Lungi Ngidi sidelined by a prior-match blow, prompting inclusions like Dusmantha Chameera and Kyle Jamieson. Arun Jaitley Stadium's flat pitch no. 5 promises batting fireworks with short boundaries and true bounce, as evidenced by recent 210-run GT innings nearly chased. RCB's 20-13 head-to-head lead adds edge, with toss, dew, and hazy warm Delhi weather as pivotal factors.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 21 - April 28, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 21 12:00 PM ET to April 28, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves hold a commanding 3-1 series lead over the Nuggets entering Game 5 on their home court in Denver, but severe injuries to Anthony Edwards (left knee bone bruise, out multiple weeks) and Donte DiVincenzo (ruptured Achilles, season over) from Game 4 have drastically altered trader consensus toward the desperate Nuggets. Ayo Dosunmu's playoff-career-high 43 points powered Minnesota's 112-96 Game 4 rout despite the early blows, yet Edwards' absence removes their top scorer and defender against Nikola Jokic. Denver regains Aaron Gordon from calf tightness, leverages Ball Arena altitude and crowd for an elimination must-win, with an NBA probe into a Game 4 scuffle pending fines or suspensions that could impact lineups. Recent Timberwolves road woes and Nuggets' home playoff dominance (5-1 last postseason) further tilt implied probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester United's recent 1-0 victory over Chelsea and Harry Maguire's return from suspension have bolstered trader consensus, pricing them at 70.5% implied probability for a home win at Old Trafford against injury-plagued Brentford. Under Michael Carrick, the third-placed Red Devils need just four points from their final fixtures to secure Champions League qualification, leveraging strong home form where they've won eight of 11 Premier League meetings with the Bees. Brentford, sitting ninth and chasing Europa spots despite Keith Andrews' guidance, face absences including Rico Henry (hamstring), Vitaly Janelt (ankle), and Fabio Carvalho (knee), capping their away upset chances at 10.5%. A draw at 19.5% reflects Brentford's recent head-to-head competitiveness.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

markets.question: Will Another Driver be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' superior constructor performance has propelled teammates Kimi Antonelli and George Russell to the top of the 2026 driver standings after four Grands Prix, with Antonelli leading at 72 points from Russell's 63 following his back-to-back wins in China and Japan, plus Russell's Australia victory and consistent podiums. Trader consensus prices reflect this tight intra-team rivalry—39.5% implied probability for Russell edging Antonelli's 32.3%—fueled by their dominant qualifying pace, flawless pit stops, and minimal DNFs amid Red Bull's reliability woes sidelining Verstappen and Ferrari's inability to match Mercedes' race pace despite Leclerc's third place at 49 points. Recent comments from Antonelli signal escalating teammate tension, keeping the championship battle fiercely competitive early in the season.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil spot prices have rebounded sharply to near $96/bbl as of April 27, 2026, up over 12% from mid-month lows around $84 triggered by initial Strait of Hormuz ceasefire hopes with Iran, now clouded by escalating geopolitical tensions and supply disruption risks. This trader consensus reflects offsetting pressures from rising U.S. crude inventories—EIA reported a 1.9 million barrel build for the week ending April 17—and OPEC+ plans for gradual supply increases amid softening global demand growth forecasts near 1 million b/d for 2026. Key catalysts ahead include the April 29 EIA petroleum status report and May IEA/OPEC monthly outlooks, with resolution imminent by month-end.

title: Pistons vs. Magic
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 27 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the Magic win, the market will resolve to "Magic".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

markets.question: Jalen Suggs: Points O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 27 at 8:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jalen Suggs scores more than 3.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Jalen Suggs scores 3.5 points or fewer during the game.

eventMetadata.context_description: Orlando Magic's 2-1 series lead over top-seeded Detroit Pistons, forged by a gritty 113-105 Game 3 comeback on April 25 where Paolo Banchero and Desmond Bane combined for 50 points, sets the stage for pivotal Game 4 tonight at Kia Center. Orlando's home-court advantage and momentum from rallying after squandering a big lead bolster trader consensus, despite a thin injury report headlined by Jonathan Isaac's knee issue. Pistons counter with Cade Cunningham's elite playmaking (27 points, 11 assists in Game 2) and a dominant 60-22 regular season, though Kevin Huerter's hip soreness leaves him questionable after early Game 3 exit. Depth concerns and rest edges could swing this closely contested matchup.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire talks mediated in Islamabad, Pakistan, trader sentiment hinges on stalled direct negotiations despite recent diplomatic signals. On April 27, Iran submitted a new proposal prompting President Trump to convene his security team, including potential involvement from Vice President JD Vance, Jared Kushner, and Steve Witkoff, while Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi blamed Washington for prior failures and toured regional allies. No bilateral meeting occurred over the April 25-26 weekend as hoped, with Tehran rejecting direct engagement amid disputes over nuclear limits, ballistic missiles, Strait of Hormuz access, and sanctions. Upcoming envoy movements and ceasefire expiration could catalyze progress or escalation.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 25 - April 27, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 25 12:00 PM ET to April 27, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket has overwhelmingly backed 40-64 posts by Elon Musk from April 25 12:00 PM ET to April 27 12:00 PM ET at 100% implied probability, anchored by xtracker's live count of 35 qualifying main feed posts (tweets, quotes, reposts) as of early April 27 morning, with a consistent 18-post daily average over the near-complete 72-hour window. This reflects Elon's typical high-engagement rhythm amid ongoing cultural flashpoints like the Musk-OpenAI trial kickoff and political commentary on left-wing violence, without explosive viral surges to push beyond the range. With mere hours to resolution and no major catalysts like a SpaceX milestone or election bombshell, an upset would require an unprecedented 30+ post frenzy—realistic only if breaking news ignites his feed.

title: LoL: DN SOOPers Challengers vs Gen.G Global Academy (BO3) - LCK Challengers League Rounds 1-2
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between DN SOOPers Challengers and Gen.G Global Academy in the LCK Challengers League Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 27 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "DN SOOPers Challengers" if DN SOOPers Challengers win the match against Gen.G Global Academy.

This market will resolve to "Gen.G Global Academy" if Gen.G Global Academy win the match against DN SOOPers Challengers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 3. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both DN SOOPers Challengers and Gen.G Global Academy each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: DN SOOPers Challengers hold sixth place at 4-4 in the LCK Challengers League Rounds 1-2 double round-robin group stage, riding momentum from a 2-1 win over Hanwha Life Esports Challengers before a narrow 1-2 loss to Nongshim Esports Academy last weekend, contrasting Gen.G Global Academy's ninth-place 2-6 record marred by 0-2 sweeps to KT Rolster Challengers and Nongshim in their latest series. This BO3 under patch 26.08 and Hard Fearless Draft rules looms large for top-five Challenge Group advancement, with prior head-to-heads this split slightly favoring Gen.G despite DN's edge in current form and stable academy rosters amid no reported changes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cagliari's stunning 3-2 home victory over Atalanta in Serie A Round 34 has driven trader consensus to price the Cagliari win at virtually 100%, reflecting the confirmed final score from Unipol Domus. Pietro Mendy netted a first-half brace to put the hosts ahead, only for Gianluca Scamacca to level with a rapid double before halftime; Gennaro Borrelli's early second-half goal sealed the upset, bolstering Cagliari's relegation fight amid strong home form. Atalanta, pre-match favorites at around 55% implied probability, faltered despite their attacking threat. With official results ratified and no appeals pending, challenges like VAR reviews are negligible, cementing the market resolution.

title: Trump Today: April 25
description: This is a market over what Donald Trump will do on April 25, 2026.

markets.question: Will Trump dance today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump dances between 12:00AM ET and 11:59PM ET on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

"Dancing" is defined as deliberate, rhythmic body movement typically matched music or a beat, such as swaying, stepping, or coordinated hand or body motions. Casual gesturing, clapping, or incidental body movement will not qualify. Dancing without the presence of music or a beat will qualify.

AI-generated content, deepfakes, or altered footage will not be considered. 

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

This market will resolve based on video.

markets.question: Will Trump wear a red tie today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump wears a red tie at any point on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The color will be determined by the most visually dominant color visible in publicly available photos or videos. For example, for the tie to be considered predominantly blue, its primary color must be a shade of blue, though it may feature other colors as part of a pattern. This tie would count as "Blue": https://i.ytimg.com/vi/20w7J2JAmqw/maxresdefault.jpg

The following tie has no distinguishable predominant color, and thus would resolve to "No". https://www.usatoday.com/gcdn/authoring/authoring-images/2025/01/20/USAT/77837111007-xxx-dcmg-105-5190802.jpg

If Donald Trump does not wear a tie or if no photos or video are released from the listed date, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump talk to Xi Jinping today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual talks with Donald Trump on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump sign an executive order today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve "Yes" if Donald Trump signs an executive order on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Executive actions will not qualify toward this market’s resolution.

This market will immediately resolve "Yes" if the text of an executive order for the given day is published on the White House page for presidential actions (https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/) or the White House press pool. Mere announcements will not qualify.

If no executive order is published by 12:00 PM ET the day after the listed date of this market, this market will resolve to “No”.

In the case of ambiguity this market may remain open until it is confirmed by the federal register.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Sleepy Joe" today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 25 disrupted the event, prompting President Trump's evacuation by Secret Service as authorities detained a suspect nearby; Trump promptly posted on Truth Social at 10:29 PM ET, sharing surveillance video and photos of the response, confirming his safety. He later delivered remarks and held a press conference at the White House addressing the security breach. This high-profile disruption overshadowed other scheduled activities like press gaggles, with trader consensus reflecting verified public actions amid ongoing law enforcement investigation into motives and potential threats. No further events are confirmed for that date, though follow-up briefings could clarify resolution details.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kharg Island, Iran's critical Persian Gulf oil terminal handling about 90% of its crude exports, remains firmly under Iranian control following U.S. precision airstrikes on military targets there on April 7, which Iranian officials reported caused no infrastructure damage. The strikes stemmed from escalating tensions over Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz, prompting a U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports that has strained oil storage and operations, with satellite imagery showing active loading as recently as April 26 amid growing tanker queues. On April 27, Iran proposed reopening the strait in exchange for lifting the U.S. blockade, a diplomatic signal President Trump is reviewing with aides; traders eye potential negotiations, further military escalation, or economic pressures as pivotal to any shift in control.

title: Trump out as President by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump resigns or is removed as President or otherwise ceases to be the President of the United States for any period of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Donald Trump's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Donald Trump remains firmly entrenched in his second term as President, with no credible indications of resignation, impeachment proceedings, invocation of the 25th Amendment, or incapacitation driving the near-unanimous trader consensus at 99.9% for "No." Over the past 30 days, no notable developments—such as health announcements, congressional investigations, or White House statements—have emerged to suggest an early exit before his term ends January 20, 2029; Republican majorities in the House and Senate further insulate against impeachment conviction requiring a two-thirds Senate vote. Realistic scenarios that could still shift odds include a sudden medical emergency, explosive scandal prompting resignation, or extraordinary bipartisan action, though historical precedents for mid-term presidential removal are exceedingly rare.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

markets.description:

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certainty at 99.7% implied probability for "No," driven by persistent US-Iran standoff enforcing mutual blockades in the Strait of Hormuz, where commercial ship transits remain below 10% of pre-crisis norms—latest MarineTraffic data shows just 2-8 crossings daily as of April 23-25 versus hundreds typically. Failed ceasefire talks, Iranian warnings of indefinite restrictions, and stranded vessels with 20,000 seafarers underscore no imminent normalization, amplifying geopolitical risk premiums in oil prices, which have surged amid supply fears. With resolution looming by April 30 via IMF Portwatch's 7-day transit average, tail risks include a surprise diplomatic deal or de-escalation sparking a late traffic surge, though entrenched hostilities make this improbable.

title: 2026 Men's French Open Winner
description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carlos Alcaraz's withdrawal from the 2026 French Open due to a lingering right wrist injury—announced three days ago after pulling out of Madrid—has propelled Jannik Sinner to a dominant 67% implied probability as trader consensus favorite, clearing his primary rival from the defending champion slate. Sinner, world No. 1, bolstered his clay credentials by capturing the 2026 Monte-Carlo Masters title over Alcaraz last week, signaling peak form on Roland Garros' surface ahead of Madrid, where he's advanced to the fourth round. Alexander Zverev (7.6%) follows as the top clay-court threat with consistent deep runs and No. 3 ranking, while Novak Djokovic (5.5%) lags amid uneven early-season results and age-related questions. French hopeful Arthur Fils (4.4%) gains traction with home support and rising momentum.

title: Which company has the best AI model end of April?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic commands a 98.4% implied probability on Polymarket for fielding the best AI model by April 30, propelled by Claude Opus 4.7's April 16 release, which vaulted it to the top of the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard with a leading Elo score around 1791—well ahead of OpenAI's GPT-5.4 at 1741 and others. This large language model excels in coding benchmarks like SWE-bench Verified (over 80%) and agentic tasks, bolstered by recent persistent memory upgrades enhancing long-context reasoning. Backed by Amazon's infrastructure, trader sentiment reflects skin-in-the-game consensus on its edge. Realistic challenges include a last-minute frontier model drop from OpenAI or Google, though typical announcement timelines and leaderboard stability make overtakes improbable in the final days.
